articles.title: Florida’s Immigration Crackdown, Led by DeSantis, Is Showing Cracks
articles.summary: Some conservative sheriffs have raised concerns about the aggressive enforcement tactics that Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican, has embraced.

articles.title: Senate votes to fund much of DHS, minus immigration enforcement
articles.summary: The Senate approved a bill to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security, early Friday. The bill does not fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement, or ICE.

articles.title: Senate Votes to Fund Most of D.H.S. in Bid to End Partial Shutdown
articles.summary: The bill excludes funding for ICE and the Border Patrol but restores it for federal airport security workers. The House could consider the package on Friday.

articles.title: Rubio Expected to Press Allies Over Strait of Hormuz at G7 Meeting
articles.summary: The meeting in France is slated to discuss efforts to stop the war in the Middle East, end Iran’s nuclear and ballistic missile development, and reopen maritime trade routes.

articles.title: A Breakthrough for T.S.A. Funding, and How the War in Iran Will Hit Grocery Prices
articles.summary: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

articles.title: Scientists watch sperm whales work as a team to assist a birth
articles.summary: An unprecedented look at the birth of a sperm whale found that mother and calf were supported by other whales throughout the process.

articles.title: Rubio heads to Europe to try garner support for Iran war
articles.summary: Representatives of the world's wealthiest democracies gather in France today for a G7 meeting, where U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is expected to try to shore up support among reticent allies fo

articles.title: UAE and Qatar Arrest Hundreds Over Online Videos of Iranian Attacks
articles.summary: The authorities have made a wave of arrests to stop people posting footage of strikes, citing security risks. Experts also see a fear of damage to the countries’ image as safe havens.

articles.title: Oil Prices Continue to Climb After Trump Delays Bombing Threat
articles.summary: President Trump said on Thursday he would delay an imminent deadline to begin attacking Iran’s power grid.

articles.title: Hegseth Strikes Two Black and Two Female Officers From Promotion List
articles.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s highly unusual decision to remove officers from a one-star promotion list has spurred allegations of racial and gender bias.

articles.title: Will ICE Change Under Its New Leader?
articles.summary: Markwayne Mullin, the new homeland security secretary, has promised a different approach, but how much change is likely? Our reporter Hamed Aleaziz describes what we know.

articles.title: Our quiz writer made yet another Tom Bombadil reference this week. Can you spot it?
articles.summary: How well do you know your "Lord of the Rings"? What about AI, Washington landmarks and TSA wait times? Find out!

articles.title: These trees brought a fishery back from the brink. They can help you too
articles.summary: A community fishery in Cambodia was struggling. There weren't enough fish to make ends meet, until local fishermen started planting a specific type of tree.

articles.title: Do you lean optimistic or pessimistic? Take this quiz and find out
articles.summary: A glass-half-full outlook can keep you engaged and hopeful in hard times. Take this quiz to find out your level of optimism, then learn how to train yourself to become more optimistic.

articles.title: Cuban Americans face scrutiny for sending goods back to the island
articles.summary: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.

articles.title: Kansas City nun reflects on life spent caring for kids
articles.summary: For StoryCorps, a nun in Kansas City reminisces about helping families in need of low-cost childcare.

articles.title: Rubio meets with other G7 nations for first time since Iran war
articles.summary: Secretary of State Rubio meets his G7 colleagues in Paris for the first time since the start of the war on Iran.

articles.title: Judge weighs whether Venezuela can pay Maduro's legal costs in US drug trafficking case
articles.summary: A U.S. judge pressed the Trump administration Thursday about its basis for barring Venezuela's government from paying former President Nicolás Maduro's legal fees in the drug trafficking case that has

articles.title: Southeast Asia is being hit hard by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas
articles.summary: Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.

articles.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
articles.summary: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.

articles.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
articles.summary: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.

articles.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.

articles.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
articles.summary: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
